engineGuide.h2: Mercedes Benz SLK Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Mercedes Benz SLK across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.1L/2.2L Diesel
entries.history: Introduced in the R170 220 CDI, replacing older diesels with refined common-rail injection and improved efficiency.
compatibleVariants: SLK 200 CDI, SLK 220 CDI, SLK 220 CDI Sport, SLK 220 CDI SE
commonFailures: Swirl flap actuator failure and vacuum line cracks (~70k-90k miles)
EGR cooler cracking and exhaust gas recirculation faults
Injector copper seal leaks causing combustion gas escape
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM646 Mercedes Benz SLK engine replacement

entries.title: 2.1L Diesel
entries.history: Mercedes flagship four-cylinder diesel, adopted across the R172 SLK range for superior torque and Euro 5 compliance.
compatibleVariants: SLK 200 CDI, SLK 220 CDI BlueEFFICIENCY, SLK 250 CDI, SLK 250 CDI AMG Line, SLK 250 CDI AMG Sport
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner weakness causing chain stretch (~60k-80k miles)
Balance shaft bearing wear leading to excessive vibration
High-pressure fuel pump internal wear and pressure drops
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM651 Mercedes Benz SLK engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: Smooth 3.0L V6 diesel introduced for R172 SLK, delivering exceptional torque and refinement for grand touring.
compatibleVariants: SLK 350 CDI BlueEFFICIENCY, SLK 350 CDI AMG Sport
commonFailures: Oil cooler and heat exchanger seal leaks (~85k-100k miles)
EGR valve sticking and carbon buildup on intake runners
Turbocharger vane actuator motor failure and boost loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM642 Mercedes Benz SLK engine replacement

entries.title: 2.2L Diesel
entries.history: Early-generation CDI unit for the R170, pioneering Mercedes common-rail technology in the compact roadster platform.
compatibleVariants: SLK 220 CDI (early pre-facelift), SLK 220 CDI Classic
commonFailures: Injection pump timing gear wear causing start issues (~70k+ miles)
Cylinder head cracking from thermal stress in tight engine bay
Turbo boost hose degradation and intercooler pipe splits
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM611 Mercedes Benz SLK engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L/2.3L Petrol
entries.history: Legendary roots engine featuring an Eaton supercharger and aluminium block, defining the early SLK sporty character.
compatibleVariants: SLK 200 Kompressor, SLK 200 Kompressor Sport, SLK 200 Kompressor SE, SLK 230 Kompressor, SLK 230 Kompressor Sport
commonFailures: Supercharger pulley clutch bearing failure and belt slippage (~80k-100k miles)
Intercooler coolant pipe cracking causing overheating
Crankcase ventilation diaphragm rupture and oil consumption
entries.cta: Get quotes for M111 Mercedes Benz SLK engine replacement

entries.title: 3.2L Petrol
entries.history: Mercedes reliable iron-block V6, bringing smooth power delivery and proven durability to the early SLK 320 model.
compatibleVariants: SLK 320, SLK 320 AMG Line, SLK 320 Sport
commonFailures: Dual mass flywheel wear causing clutch judder (~90k+ miles)
Camshaft position sensor failure leading to misfires
Valley cover oil seepage from degraded gasket seals
entries.cta: Get quotes for M112 Mercedes Benz SLK engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol
entries.history: Compact supercharged turbo four-cylinder that powered the bulk of R171/R172 SLKs, prioritising agility and fuel economy.
compatibleVariants: SLK 200 Kompressor, SLK 200 CGI BlueEFFICIENCY, SLK 250 CGI, SLK 250 AMG Line
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch and guide rail failure (critical, ~50k-80k miles)
Camshaft adjuster sprocket wear causing cold-start rattle
Oil sludge accumulation from delayed service intervals
entries.cta: Get quotes for M271 Mercedes Benz SLK engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Replaced the M271 in late R172 models, offering improved turbo response, reduced friction, and stricter emissions compliance.
compatibleVariants: SLK 250, SLK 250 Edition 1, SLK 250 AMG Line Plus
commonFailures: Coolant leak into combustion chamber via cylinder head gasket (~70k-90k miles)
Turbocharger bearing wear and wastegate rattle
Direct injection intake valve carbon buildup causing rough idle
entries.cta: Get quotes for M274 Mercedes Benz SLK engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L/3.5L Petrol
entries.history: Smooth aluminium V6 replacing M112, featuring variable timing but infamous for critical balance shaft gear failure.
compatibleVariants: SLK 280, SLK 350, SLK 350 Sport, SLK 350 AMG Sport, SLK 350 Black Series
commonFailures: Balance shaft gear tooth wear leading to catastrophic failure (~60k-100k miles)
Camshaft timing chain tensioner oil leak and chain slack
Intake manifold swirl flap motor failure and vacuum loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for M272 Mercedes Benz SLK engine replacement

entries.title: 5.4L Petrol
entries.history: Hand-built naturally aspirated V8 that transformed the SLK into a genuine AMG performance roadster.
compatibleVariants: SLK 55 AMG, SLK 55 AMG AMG Performance Package
commonFailures: Piston ring wear and increased oil consumption from sustained high RPM use (~90k+ miles)
Valve stem seal hardening causing blue exhaust smoke on startup
Crankshaft main bearing fatigue and oil pressure drops
entries.cta: Get quotes for M113 Mercedes Benz SLK engine replacement

entries.title: 5.5L Petrol
entries.history: Advanced 5.5L NA V8 featuring dry sump lubrication, lightweight block, and rapid throttle response for track duty.
compatibleVariants: SLK 55 AMG, SLK 55 AMG Edition 1, SLK 55 AMG CarbonLook, SLK 55 AMG GT
commonFailures: High-pressure fuel pump internal seal failure and lean running (~80k miles)
Exhaust manifold cracking under extreme thermal cycling
Oil filter housing and cooler gasket degradation
entries.cta: Get quotes for M152 Mercedes Benz SLK engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: World’s most powerful production 2.0L turbo at launch, delivering explosive acceleration in the limited SLK 45 AMG.
compatibleVariants: SLK 45 AMG, SLK 45 AMG Edition 1, SLK 45 AMG Pro
commonFailures: Turbocharger wastegate actuator rattle and boost inconsistency (~60k miles)
Piston ringland stress fractures from aggressive tuning and track use
Electric intercooler coolant pump failure and heat soak
entries.cta: Get quotes for M133 Mercedes Benz SLK engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Mercedes Benz SLK Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.group: Mercedes Benz SLK M272 Balance Shaft Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz SLK M272 Balance Shaft Failure - Why V6 Engines Suffer Catastrophic Timing Loss
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz SLK 300, SLK 350, SLK 350 AMG Sport (2004-2011, M272.960, M272.967, M272.975)
problems.rootCause: Early-production M272 V6 engines featured a hardened steel balance shaft gear that wore prematurely against a softer composite sprocket. Once the gear teeth skip, timing alignment is lost, triggering P1200/P1208 fault codes and often causing bent valves or complete engine seizure.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Early Symptom Check & Code Clear
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic scan, oil pressure test, and temporary EML reset
repairOptions.longevity: Only buys a few hundred miles; does not stop mechanical wear

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Balance Shaft Gear & Chain Kit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine removal, balance shaft gear replacement, updated tensioners, full timing re-alignment
repairOptions.longevity: Extends engine life 30k-50k miles; assumes no internal valve damage

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned M272 V6
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of upgraded rebuilt V6 with revised balance shaft, new timing kit, gaskets, and 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent resolution; eliminates all hidden wear and restores factory compression

problems.recommendation: If your SLK 350 is already throwing P1200 codes or running rough, the internal timing jump has likely occurred and a repair kit will not save it. We recommend a full rebuilt M272 with the updated steel sprocket, as the cost differential against an in-engine repair is narrow once labour for engine removal is factored in.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M272 Mercedes Benz SLK engine prices

problems.group: Mercedes Benz SLK M271 Timing Chain Stretch
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz SLK M271 Timing Chain Stretch - Why Petrol 1.8L & 2.0L Engines Rattle on Start
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz SLK 200 Kompressor, SLK 200 CGI, SLK 250 CGI (2004-2015, M271.948, M271.955, 271.860, 271.861)
problems.rootCause: The M271’s plastic timing guide rails become brittle under sustained heat cycles, while the hydraulic tensioner loses pressure over time. This allows the single-row chain to stretch beyond tolerance, causing the notorious cold-start rattle, camshaft adjuster misfires, and eventual valve timing loss.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Cam Adjuster & Tensioner Swap
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Front cover access, updated tensioner install, cam phaser replacement, chain inspection
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if chain stretch is under 2mm; catches wear before catastrophic jump

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Full Timing Chain Kit & Rail Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine-out procedure, new chain, hardened rails, water pump, tensioner, and cam adjusters
repairOptions.longevity: Restores timing integrity for 60k-80k miles; requires pristine cylinder bores

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt M271 with upgraded chain tensioner, reinforced guides, bench-tested and warranted
repairOptions.longevity: Economically superior once internal scoring is present; includes updated M271 Evo components

problems.recommendation: Listen for the cold-start rattle. If the chain has already jumped a tooth or triggered camshaft position codes, do not risk a kit install on a compromised block. For SLK 200 and 250 owners, a rebuilt M271 with reinforced timing components offers better long-term value and a warranty that a partial repair cannot match.
problems.cta: Get quotes for M271 Mercedes Benz SLK engine replacement

problems.group: Mercedes Benz SLK OM651 Timing Chain & Tensioner Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz SLK OM651 Timing Chain & Tensioner Failure - Why Diesel CDI Units Lose Compression
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz SLK 200 CDI, SLK 220 CDI BlueEFFICIENCY, SLK 250 CDI (2009-2015, OM651.980, 651.910, 651.940)
problems.rootCause: The OM651’s rear-mounted timing chain tensioner relies on consistent oil pressure to maintain preload. Sludge buildup from delayed servicing or weak oil pump output allows the chain to slacken, leading to guide rail wear, excessive chain stretch, and potential piston-to-valve contact at high RPM.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Tensioner & Oil Filter Service
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rear tensioner replacement, oil filter housing seal, full synthetic flush, ECU service reset
repairOptions.longevity: Stops early slackening; only viable if no chain stretch or DPF contamination

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine removal, complete chain kit, updated tensioner, rail replacement, compression test
repairOptions.longevity: Reliable if cylinder scoring is absent; labour-intensive on R172 platform

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt OM651 CDI Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of reconditioned OM651 with heavy-duty tensioner, new DPF/EGR service, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best solution for high-mileage SLK diesels; resolves all chain, turbo, and injector wear

problems.recommendation: Diesel owners should prioritise strict 10k-mile service intervals. Once the tensioner has failed and the chain has stretched past tolerance, a rebuild is significantly safer and more cost-effective than retrofitting a chain kit into a block with potential micro-scoring. We advise a full rebuilt OM651 for any SLK 250 CDI approaching 80k miles with rattling symptoms.
problems.cta: Compare OM651 Mercedes Benz SLK diesel engine prices

problems.group: Mercedes Benz SLK 55 AMG V8 Valve Seal & Piston Ring Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz SLK 55 AMG V8 Valve Seal & Piston Ring Failure - Why High-Performance Units Burn Oil
problems.rootCause: Sustained high-RPM operation and aggressive throttle cycling degrade the M113/M152’s exhaust valve stem seals and accelerate piston ring wear. This leads to significant blue smoke on startup, elevated oil consumption (1L per 1,000 miles), and eventual catalytic converter fouling from oil ash.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Valve Cover & PCV Service
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Crankcase ventilation overhaul, updated valve cover gaskets, PCV diaphragm replacement, oil analysis
repairOptions.longevity: Manages minor oil weep; does not address internal ring or seal degradation

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head removal, new viton valve seals, head gasket, piston ring inspection, dyno tune
repairOptions.longevity: Restores compression temporarily; risk of recurring wear on bottom end remains

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt AMG 5.5L V8 Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of specialist AMG-spec rebuilt V8, forged internals, dry-sump service, full warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only definitive fix for track-driven or high-mileage AMG roadsters; guarantees performance output

problems.recommendation: For the SLK 55 AMG, partial repairs rarely deliver long-term results given the engine’s high thermal and mechanical loads. If oil consumption exceeds 0.5L per 500 miles or catalytic fault codes appear, a specialist rebuilt M152 or M113 with upgraded piston rings is the only financially sound route to preserve the car’s value.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt AMG Mercedes Benz SLK 55 V8 engine quotes

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mercedes Benz SLK
finalCta.paragraph: The SLK’s compact engine bay and high-revving performance nature mean main dealer repairs quickly escalate into write-off territory. EngineMarket removes the guesswork by connecting you directly with vetted UK Mercedes Benz SLK engine specialists who understand these precise failure patterns. Every quoted replacement includes upgraded components that address the original design weakness, bench-tested performance, and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and keep your roadster on the road without paying dealer premiums.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mercedes Benz SLK Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mercedes Benz SLK and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Mercedes Benz SLK Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement officially fitted to the Mercedes Benz SLK across all UK generations is detailed below, with compatible engine codes, production years, and documented failure notes to help you identify the correct replacement unit.

groups.title: Mercedes Benz SLK Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz SLK 2.1L Diesel Engines
items.description: Introduced during the R171 facelift and standardised across the R172 generation, the 2.1L four‑cylinder turbo diesel became the backbone of the SLK’s efficiency‑focused lineup. Featuring common‑rail injection and BlueEFFICIENCY technology, it delivered strong low‑end torque while meeting strict Euro 5 emissions standards. This displacement was heavily favoured by UK daily drivers seeking a practical, high‑MPG roadster.
commonFailurePoints: DPF regeneration issues at urban mileage.
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz SLK 2.1L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz SLK 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: Fitted exclusively to the late‑stage R172 generation, the 3.0L V6 turbo diesel was engineered as a premium grand‑touring powertrain for the SLK 350 CDI. It paired an aluminium block with twin‑turbo geometry to deliver effortless highway cruising refinement and rapid mid‑range acceleration. Though limited in production numbers, it remains highly sought after by enthusiasts prioritising torque and long‑distance comfort.
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz SLK 3.0L diesel engines

groups.title: Mercedes Benz SLK Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz SLK 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The cornerstone of the R171 and early R172 generations, the 1.8L supercharged and turbocharged petrol unit powered the vast majority of SLK 200 and SLK 250 variants sold in the UK. Mercedes integrated direct CGI injection and an evolution of forced induction to balance responsive throttle delivery with manageable running costs. Its compact packaging made it ideal for the SLK’s tight engine bay, though it carries well‑documented timing chain vulnerabilities.
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz SLK 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz SLK 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Spanning the early R170 supercharged models and the final‑year R172 AMG‑tuned variants, the 2.0L petrol displacement represents both Mercedes’ original lightweight sports car philosophy and its modern high‑output turbo engineering. Later iterations utilised direct injection and advanced turbocharging to extract AMG‑level performance from a compact four‑cylinder architecture. This size appeals to owners upgrading older units or seeking the rare M133 AMG specification.
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz SLK 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz SLK 2.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: Featured exclusively in the first‑generation R170 roadster, the 2.3L supercharged four‑cylinder established the SLK’s reputation for accessible, high‑revving performance. It combined a robust iron‑block design with an Eaton M45 supercharger to deliver linear power delivery and a distinctive mechanical soundtrack. While largely replaced by smaller turbo units in later generations, it remains a favourite among classic Mercedes roadster collectors.
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz SLK 2.3L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz SLK 3.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: Serving as the premium six‑cylinder option for the original R170 chassis, the 3.2L V6 was engineered for smooth, vibration‑free power delivery and effortless motorway cruising. Its proven iron‑block architecture and multi‑point fuel injection system offered exceptional durability, making it one of the most reliable engines fitted to the early SLK lineup. UK owners typically chose this displacement for long‑term touring rather than track‑focused performance.
commonFailurePoints: Dual mass flywheel wear causing clutch judder
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz SLK 3.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz SLK 3.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: Introduced as the flagship V6 for the R171 and continued through the R172, the 3.5L naturally aspirated petrol unit represented Mercedes’ push toward higher specific output and refined acoustic tuning. Featuring variable valve timing and a lightweight aluminium block, it delivered sharp throttle response and a linear power curve that suited the SLK’s sports car dynamics perfectly. This displacement dominates UK replacement enquiries due to its widespread use and known balance shaft vulnerabilities in early production runs.
commonFailurePoints: Balance shaft gear tooth wear causing timing loss
cam cover sealing disc oil leaks
intake manifold swirl flap motor failure.
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz SLK 3.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz SLK 5.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: Reserved exclusively for the AMG‑badged SLK 55 variants across both the R171 and R172 generations, the 5.5L V8 represents the pinnacle of Mercedes‑Benz roadster performance engineering. Hand‑built at the Affalterbach facility, these naturally aspirated and later dry‑sump units deliver explosive acceleration, track‑ready thermal management, and a signature AMG exhaust note. Their extreme output and high mechanical stresses necessitate specialist rebuilding with upgraded bottom‑end components for longevity. Not sure which engine size your Mercedes Benz SLK needs? Enter your registration number above - we’ll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Mercedes Benz SLK specialists.
commonFailurePoints: Piston ring land stress fractures from sustained high‑RPM use
exhaust manifold cracking under thermal cycling
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz SLK 5.5L petrol engine prices

items.description: Diesel SLK models span the R171 facelift and full R172 generation, offering efficient four‑cylinder and V6 options that dominate high‑mileage replacement enquiries.
items.descriptor: Diesel SLK models span the R171 facelift and full R172 generation, offering efficient four‑cylinder and V6 options that dominate high‑mileage replacement enquiries.

items.descriptor: Petrol power defines the SLK range across all three UK generations, from supercharged four‑cylinders to hand‑built AMG V8s, with timing chain and balance shaft issues driving most replacement searches.

engineYears.h2: Mercedes Benz SLK Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers every major engine change across the Mercedes Benz SLK's UK production run (1996-2016), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine code and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: R170 Launch: Supercharged Four-Cylinder & V6 Debut
years.description: The original R170 SLK launched with supercharged M111 four-cylinders and the smooth M112 V6, establishing the model's sports roadster credentials.
keyChanges: First-generation R170 platform introduced with retractable hardtop (Vario-roof)
M111 Kompressor supercharged engines debut with Eaton M45 blower
M112 3.2L V6 added as premium option for refined performance
UK market receives right-hand drive SLK 200, 230 Kompressor and 320 variants
knownFor: Robust iron-block M111 engines with distinctive supercharger whine
M112 V6 praised for smoothness and long-term reliability
Supercharger clutch wear and intercooler hose splits common at 80k+ miles
Early R170 models now sought by classic Mercedes roadster enthusiasts
years.cta: Get quotes for 1996-2000 Mercedes Benz SLK replacement engines
years.ticker: SLK 230 Kompressor engine replacement • M111 supercharger failure • SLK 320 V6 engine cost

years.description: The closing phase of the R170 generation saw refined tuning and widespread UK adoption before the all-new R171 arrived.
keyChanges: Minor ECU and emissions updates for Euro 3 compliance
Increased standard equipment across SLK 200 and 230 Kompressor trims
Final production year 2004 marked transition to next-generation R171
Used SLK 230 and 320 models remain popular entry points to Mercedes ownership
knownFor: Well-maintained R170 units offer excellent value as classic sports cars
Timing chain guides on M111 can wear; listen for cold-start rattle
M112 V6 remains one of Mercedes' most dependable engines
Replacement demand driven by enthusiasts restoring early SLK models
years.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz SLK 2001-2004 engine prices

years.preview: R171 Launch: New Platform, New Engine Families
years.description: The all-new R171 SLK introduced the M271 supercharged four-cylinder and the M272 V6, alongside the hand-built M113 AMG V8 for the flagship SLK 55.
keyChanges: All-new R171 chassis with improved rigidity and refined Vario-roof mechanism
M271 1.8L supercharged engine replaces M111 as the core four-cylinder unit
M272 3.5L V6 debuts with variable valve timing and aluminium construction
M113 5.5L V8 hand-built by AMG for the new SLK 55 performance model
knownFor: M272 early-production units carry balance shaft gear failure risk (pre-2008)
M271 timing chain stretch emerges as key failure point from 60k miles
M113 AMG V8 delivers explosive performance but requires specialist maintenance
First diesel SLK (OM646) offers strong economy but swirl flap issues reported
years.cta: Get quotes for 2004-2007 Mercedes Benz SLK replacement engines
years.ticker: SLK 350 balance shaft failure • M271 timing chain replacement • SLK 55 AMG V8 rebuild cost

years.preview: R171 Facelift: M271 Evo & OM651 Diesel Arrive
years.description: The mid-cycle R171 update introduced revised M271 Evo engines and the new OM651 diesel, while addressing some early M272 balance shaft concerns.
keyChanges: Facelifted R171 with updated styling, LED daytime running lights and interior revisions
M271.955 Evo engine introduced with improved timing components and CGI direct injection option
OM651 2.1L diesel replaces OM646 across SLK 200/220/250 CDI variants
M272 balance shaft gear revised on later production SLK 350 units (post-2008)
mainEngines: M271.955 - 1.8L turbo petrol with CGI (SLK 200 CGI, SLK 250 CGI)
M272.967 - 3.5L V6 with revised balance shaft (SLK 350 facelift)
OM651.980 - 2.1L diesel with improved injection (SLK 200/220/250 CDI)
knownFor: M271 Evo still vulnerable to timing chain stretch; preventative replacement recommended
OM651 timing chain tensioner weakness emerges as key failure point at 70k-100k miles
Post-2008 M272 units less prone to balance shaft failure but not immune
R171 facelift models represent sweet spot for used SLK buyers seeking modern features

years.ticker: SLK 250 CDI OM651 engine • M271 cam sprocket failure • SLK 350 P1200 fault code

years.preview: R172 Launch: All-New Generation, New Engine Architecture
years.description: The third-generation R172 SLK brought a lighter chassis, new M274/M276 engines and the powerful OM642 V6 diesel option.
keyChanges: All-new R172 platform with aluminium-intensive construction and improved aerodynamics
M274 2.0L turbo petrol replaces M271 in SLK 250, featuring direct injection and reduced friction
M276 3.5L V6 replaces M272 in SLK 350, with revised timing drive and improved reliability
OM642 3.0L V6 diesel introduced for SLK 350 CDI, delivering strong torque and refinement
MAGIC SKY CONTROL panoramic roof option debuts on higher trims

faq.h2: Mercedes Benz SLK Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Mercedes Benz SLK engine UK?
items.answer: Mercedes Benz SLK engine replacement typically costs £1,500-£4,800 for rebuilt units supply-only, depending on engine code and variant. Diesel OM651 and petrol M271 units sit at the lower end, while M272 V6 and AMG M113/M152 engines command premium pricing due to complexity and specialist rebuild requirements.
keyPoints: Rebuilt M271 1.8L petrol engines range £1,700-£3,500 supply-only UK
M272 3.5L V6 replacement costs £2,500-£4,800 depending on balance shaft upgrade
AMG SLK 55 V8 engines require specialist rebuilders, pricing £3,500-£8,500
Supply & fit labour typically adds £450-£850 to engine price
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Mercedes Benz SLK engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: Is the M272 V6 in the Mercedes Benz SLK 350 reliable?
items.answer: Early M272 V6 engines (pre-2008) in the Mercedes Benz SLK 350 suffer from balance shaft gear failure, a known catastrophic issue. Post-2008 units received revised components and are significantly more reliable, though regular oil changes remain critical for longevity.
keyPoints: Balance shaft gear wear affects M272 engines built before late 2007
P1200/P1208 fault codes indicate timing misalignment requiring urgent attention
Revised M272 engines from 2008 onwards show markedly improved durability
Regular synthetic oil changes every 10k miles extend M272 lifespan significantly
items.cta: Get quotes for revised M272 Mercedes Benz SLK 350 replacement engines

items.question: What causes the M271 timing chain to fail on Mercedes Benz SLK 200 and 250?
items.answer: M271 timing chain failure on Mercedes Benz SLK 200/250 stems from plastic guide rail degradation and hydraulic tensioner wear, typically appearing between 60k-85k miles. The characteristic cold-start rattle is the earliest warning sign before catastrophic timing jump occurs.
keyPoints: Plastic timing guide rails become brittle under repeated heat cycles
Hydraulic tensioner pressure loss allows chain stretch beyond safe tolerance
Camshaft adjuster sprocket wear accelerates timing component deterioration
Preventative chain kit replacement costs less than post-failure engine rebuild
items.cta: Compare M271 timing chain repair versus rebuilt engine costs for your SLK

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Mercedes Benz SLK?
items.answer: Replacing a Mercedes Benz SLK engine is worthwhile if the chassis, transmission, suspension and interior remain in good structural and cosmetic condition. A rebuilt engine with warranty typically costs £1,500-£4,800 supply-only, far less than purchasing a comparable low-mileage replacement vehicle, making engine replacement the economical choice for enthusiast owners keeping their roadster long-term.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines include upgraded components addressing original failure points
SLK roadsters retain strong enthusiast demand when mechanically sound
Supply & fit services available nationwide from Mercedes Benz SLK specialists
12-month unlimited mileage warranty protects your investment post-replacement
items.cta: Get a tailored quote to replace your Mercedes Benz SLK engine today

items.question: Which Mercedes Benz SLK engine is most reliable?
items.answer: The M112 3.2L V6 in early R170 Mercedes Benz SLK 320 models ranks as the most reliable engine, thanks to its robust iron block and simple multi-point injection. Later M276 3.5L V6 units also demonstrate strong longevity when properly maintained with regular synthetic oil changes.
keyPoints: M112 V6 engines rarely suffer catastrophic failures with basic maintenance
M276 engines benefit from revised timing drive versus problematic M272 design
OM651 diesel units offer strong mileage but require DPF-aware driving patterns
Avoid early M272 V6 and M271 petrol engines without timing component inspection verification
items.cta: Find reliable reconditioned Mercedes Benz SLK engines with UK warranty coverage

items.question: How long do Mercedes Benz SLK engines last?
items.answer: With proper maintenance, Mercedes Benz SLK engines typically reach 120k-180k miles before major overhaul. M112 and M276 V6 units often exceed 200k miles, while M271 and early M272 engines may require timing component attention between 60k-100k miles to achieve similar longevity.
keyPoints: Full service history with synthetic oil extends Mercedes Benz SLK engine life significantly
Timing chain components on M271/M272 engines need inspection by 70k miles
Diesel OM651 units benefit from regular DPF regeneration cycles and EGR cleaning
AMG M113/M152 V8 engines require specialist servicing but reward with strong durability
items.cta: Check rebuilt Mercedes Benz SLK engine options with 12-month warranty coverage

items.question: Can I fit a different engine in my Mercedes Benz SLK?
items.answer: Engine swaps in the Mercedes Benz SLK are complex due to tight packaging constraints, ECU coding requirements, transmission compatibility and chassis-specific mounting points. While technically possible with specialist fabrication, most owners find replacing with the original engine code more cost-effective, reliable and legally compliant than attempting cross-code conversions or performance upgrades.
keyPoints: SLK engine bays are compact, limiting fitment options for larger displacement units
ECU immobiliser systems require specialist coding when swapping engine codes
Transmission compatibility and driveshaft alignment must be verified before any swap
Original-code rebuilt engines preserve vehicle value and simplify UK MOT compliance
items.cta: Compare original-spec rebuilt Mercedes Benz SLK engines for straightforward replacement

items.question: What's the difference between reconditioned and used Mercedes Benz SLK engines?
items.answer: Reconditioned Mercedes Benz SLK engines undergo strip-down inspection with worn components replaced and bench testing, while used engines are removed from donor vehicles with minimal refurbishment. Reconditioned units include warranty coverage and upgraded failure-point components, offering better long-term value despite higher initial cost.
keyPoints: Reconditioned engines receive new pistons, bearings, gaskets and timing components
Used engines carry unknown mileage and hidden wear risks without warranty protection
Reconditioned M271/M272 units include revised timing parts addressing known failures
Both options available supply-only or with nationwide specialist fitment services
items.cta: Compare reconditioned versus used Mercedes Benz SLK engine prices and warranty terms

items.question: Do I need to code the ECU after replacing my Mercedes Benz SLK engine?
items.answer: Most Mercedes Benz SLK engine replacements require ECU adaptation or coding to match the new unit's serial data, particularly on post-2004 models with immobiliser systems. Specialist fitting services include this coding step, ensuring the engine starts and runs correctly without dashboard warning lights.
keyPoints: Post-2004 SLK models use immobiliser-linked ECUs requiring serial number adaptation
M272 and M271 engines often need camshaft position relearning after installation
Diesel OM651 units may require DPF regeneration reset following engine swap
UK specialist fitters include ECU coding in supply & fit service packages
items.cta: Find Mercedes Benz SLK engine supply & fit specialists who handle ECU coding

items.question: Where can I find the engine code on my Mercedes Benz SLK?
items.answer: The engine code on your Mercedes Benz SLK appears on the engine block stamping, vehicle V5C registration document, and under-bonnet identification plate. Common codes include M271 for SLK 200, M272 for SLK 350, and OM651 for diesel variants - enter your registration above for instant verification.
keyPoints: Engine code stamped on block near timing cover or cylinder head
V5C registration document lists engine code in section D.2
Under-bonnet VIN plate includes engine type designation for UK-market SLK models
Registration lookup instantly confirms your exact Mercedes Benz SLK engine code
items.cta: Enter your registration to identify your Mercedes Benz SLK engine and compare quotes
